WerecommendedandtheManagementagreedto conduct the one-time cleansing of PPE by performing the following: a. Identify other departments within the LGU that may have available staff with relevant skills (e.g., record-keeping, accounting, or logistics) who can be temporarily reassigned to assist and focus with the inventory process. b. Collaborate with other government agencies that can provide training on inventory management, especially on the full implementation of the 'One-time Cleansing of PPE Accounts,' as outlined in COA Circular 2020-006, so that the LGU may be fully aware of the primary guidelines of the said circular. c. EstablishanewsetofInventoryCommittee, with members temporarily relieved of their regular duties until the completion of the physical inventory within a clear and agreed-upon timeline. d. Ensure that the newly reconstituted Inventory Committee prepare and submit the approved Physical Inventory Plan to the COA Audit Team at least 10 calendar days before the scheduled start of the inventory-taking process.

WerecommendedandtheManagementagreed to: a. Institute appropriate legal sanction against concerned officials or employees, by withholding or suspension of their salaries; and allowing personnel to make another cash advances , unless previous was liquidated

WerecommendedandtheManagementagreed to: a. Strictly observe the standards on the preparation of the FS particularly on the restatement of balances by restating the subsequent year’s FS to reflect the adjustments made for prior period errors and disclosing the same in the Notes to the FS; and b. Revisit the IPSAS and other related COA issuances, particularly on the preparation and presentation of restated FS.

WerecommendedandtheManagementagreed to: a. Strictly comply with COA Circular No. 97-002 and Section 93 of Presidential Decree No. 1445, which require that all government payments be made either by check, payable directly to the creditor or supplier or cash advances granted only to authorized disbursing officers for specific, approved purposes; b. Discontinue the current practice of reimbursing municipal officials or employees who made payments in cash, as it violates prescribed government disbursement procedures; c. Require that only designated disbursing officers handle cash payments, and such cash payments must be made exclusively through properly approved cash advances; and d. Ensure that all units of the LGU follow proper procurement processes in accordance with existing laws and regulations.

WerecommendedandtheManagementagreed to: a. Comply with the operational timelines specified in the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 for all future procurement processes, specifically on the minimum 20-calendar-day duration specified in the law; and b. Implement and attend continuous training programs for all personnel involved in procurement processes to ensure a thorough understanding of the legal requirements under RA No. 9184, particularly periodic workshops on procurement best practices, compliance standards, and the importance of maintaining transparency and accountability.

WerecommendedandtheManagementagreed to: Adhere the guidelines and provisions for the utilization of the DRRMF, particularly those outlined in DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-73, dated April 17, 2012, and Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1, dated March 25, 2013, issued by the NDRRMC, DBM, and DILG. Additionally, a regular monitoring and review mechanism should be established to ensure that funds allocated for disaster preparedness are used appropriately and in accordance with the prescribed guidelines, and thereby preventing any deviation from their intended purpose.

WerecommendedandtheManagementagreed to: a. Conduct a comprehensive review of requests from employees intending to monetize 50% or more of their accrued leave credits (VL & SL). This entails demanding valid and justifiable reasons and ensuring compliance with additional requirements outlined in Section 23 of the Omnibus Rule before authorizing any disbursements; and, integrating these strict definitions and requirements into a new Internal Policy Addendum and a revised request form. b. Require the personnel listed above to furnish justification and verifiable evidence demonstrating that the proceeds were utilized for purposes outlined in the Omnibus Rule; and, making the HR Head responsible for certifying that all requests for monetizing 50% or more of leave credits meet these stringent compliance requirements before final authorization for disbursement is granted. c. Moving forward, ensure compliance with Rule XVI of the Omnibus Rules Implementing Book V of E.O. No. 292 requirements per COA 2012-001, 5.14 (Payments for Monetization).

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the LGU’s Inventory Team fast track and complete the PPE inventory count to verify the existence of the LGU’s properties and submit the required report to the Audit Team. Moreover, the inventory report should be reconciled with the accounting and property records. Any discrepancies found should be investigated.

We recommended that Management: The Municipal Accountant:
a. Exert effort in obtaining unserviceable records/documents pertaining to the acquisition of the unrecorded properties and direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the adjusting entries to record the aforementioned properties.
b. Require the Inventory Team to fast track the inventory taking and improve its property and supply management by disposing of unserviceable properties in accordance with pertinent laws, rules, and regulations.
c. Direct the Municipal Accountant to consider the impairment of these unserviceable assets as well as those determined by the inventory team to be unserviceable beyond repair and recognize the corresponding impairment losses and allowance for impairment.

We recommended and Management agreed to: review the receivables schedules and make the necessary adjusting entries and to:

a. Direct and ensure that the Municipal Accountant:
1. Analyze, review, reconcile and monitor all receivables and adjust the books to ensure that only valid receivables were recorded in the books.
2. File a request for write-off if warranted and following the COA Circular 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016, if the concerned debtors can no longer pay due to insolvency, can no longer be located, already deceased or no records/documents available to validate claim.

b. Exert all efforts in collecting these claims by deducting these claims from their salaries for those who are still in the government.

We recommended and Management agreed to direct and ensure that the:

a. Municipal Treasurer prepare a schedule of unreleased/unclaimed checks at the end of the reporting period and submit the same thereafter to the Municipal Accountant; and

b. Municipal Accountant to post adjustment in the books of accounts that correspond to the unreleased or unclaimed checks at the end of the reporting period.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Accountant to compute and make necessary adjusting entry to capitalize the borrowing costs to present faithfully the balances of the said accounts. Thereafter, submit a copy of the Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to the Office of the Audit Team for reference.

We recommended and Management agreed to require the Municipal Accountant to:

a. Properly disclose the above-mentioned resolution along with EO 138, their details, financial, and non-financial effects to the LGU;

b. Revise the account name of the account “Share from Internal Revenue Allotment” to “Share from National Tax Allotment” in compliance with the above-mentioned resolution and EO; and

c. Moving forward, to study constantly the International Federation of Accountants (IFAC) publications, COA publications and accounting rules and regulations and consult with the audit team.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

a. Direct and ensure the Municipal Engineer monitor and implement all projects during the year to ensure the optimum utilization of the 20% Development Fund; and is nearly fully utilized.

b. Strengthen and enhance project planning in the entire LGU, particularly in setting attainable timelines for project implementation to achieve the desired benefits as envisioned in Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 of DILG and DBM.

We recommended that Management to: Mandate the Municipal Assessor to process the Certificate of Title of the untitled parcels upon full payment of the same certificates of title for CY 2025, with the titling targeted for completion by 2026 due to the need for additional funding.

b. Gradually allocate funds needed to process the titling; Direct the Municipal Budget Officer to give priority to the allocation of funds for titling for the certificate of title of LGU lands will be given in tranches.

c. Direct the Municipal Assessor to facilitate and supervise the proceedings of land titling and to cause the immediate transfer of the parcels of land to the Municipality; and

d. Ensure that all future land acquisitions, whether through purchase or donation are titled in the name of the Municipality.

b. Submit the monthly monitoring report on the implementation of the PPAs using the template prescribed in the above-mentioned circular, with information as to the mode of procurement and specific status of the project such as on-going, for implementation, completed, suspended/cancelled, terminated and for bidding.

Recommendation

      We  recommend  that  concerned  management  officials  to  religiously
implement the above cited regulations on the conduct  of  periodic  physical
inventory of the LGU’s  PPE  as  well  as  to  appropriately  reconcile  the

Recommendation

      We recommend that the concerned municipal officials to  comply  with
Republic Act No. 7743 and DILG  MC  No.  2017-95,  by  appropriating  the
necessary  funds  in  establishing  the  Municipal  Reading  Center.  The
municipal mayor shall also supervise the Punong Barangay’s compliance  in
establishing the Barangay Reading Center.

Recommendation

      We recommend that the Local Chief Executive to  direct  the  Municipal
Accountant to strictly observe the rules and regulations on the  preparation
of the financial statements.

Recommendation

      We recommend that management to follow the LCPC Plan  and  Budget  and
follow the guidelines in the utilization of the LCPC  as  provided  in  DILG
Circular 2012-120 dated July 4, 2012.

Recommendation

      We  recommend  that  management  to  follow  the  guidelines  in   the
utilization of the LDRRMF as provided in COA Circular No. 2012-02.

Recommendation

      We recommend that the  Local  Chief  Executive  and  OIC-  SWMFP  to
involve closely the barangay officials in  solid  wastes  management  and
activate the Barangay Solid Waste Management Committees  for  the  strict
enforcement and continual implementation of  the  existing  environmental
laws  and  consider  extending  financial  assistance,   resources,   and
technology to the  8  barangays  which  have  remained  non-compliant  in
establishing MRFs. Moreover, continuously intensify information  campaign
and educate the constituents on the necessity of establishment of MRF  in
every barangay.

Recommendation

      We recommend that concerned LGU officials and employees should  always
be  aware  and  committed  to  implement  all  applicable  laws,  rules  and
regulations in the granting as well as the utilization of cash  advances  to
avoid possible disciplinary actions or sanctions  in  the  future  for  non-
compliance thereof. Issue a demand letter relative to the past due  accounts
to remind the  accountable  persons  of  their  duties  to  liquidate  their
advances.

Recommendation


      We recommend that  the  Municipal  Treasurer  to  timely  submit  the
related RCIs, RODs, RCDs and supporting  documents  for  all  funds  to  the
Municipal Accountant for the timely recording in the books of  accounts  and
preparation   of   the   trial   balances,   financial   statements,    bank
reconciliation statements and other related periodic reports for  submission
to the Auditor and other government regulatory bodies.

Recommendation

      We recommend that the management directs the General Services  Officer
(GSO) and the Municipal Engineer  to  submit  promptly  all  contracts  with
their complete supporting documents to the Audit Team for review  within
five days from  the  execution  of  the  contract  in  compliance  with  the
existing  regulations.  Likewise,  request  for  technical   inspection   of
completed projects must be submitted as required.

Recommendation

      We recommend that management not  to  authorize  deductions  from  the
salaries of the   employees that will reduce their net take home  pay  below
P 4,000.00. Deductions from salaries should be prioritized as  shown  above.
